title: [VectorCombine] Preserve atomicity when scalarizing vector loads
author: jlebar
description:
scalarizeLoadExtract tries to convert e.g.

  %a = load <2 x i32> %ptr
  %b = extractelement %a, 1

into a scalar load.  Problem is, if the original load is atomic, the new
load was not.

Moreover, if the original load was an ordered atomic, it would not be
DCE'd after we created the new load. The pass would repeatedly visit the
original load, hitting an infinite loop.

This bug was found by a large run of Opus 4.7 looking for bugs in LLVM.

tests.test_body:
define i32 @load_multiple_extracts_with_unique_variable_indices_large_vector_valid_by_and(ptr %x, ptr %y, i64 %idx) {
  %idx.clamped = and i64 %idx, 15
  %lx = load <16 x i32>, ptr %x, align 64
  %ly = load <16 x i32>, ptr %y, align 64
  %e.0 = extractelement <16 x i32> %lx, i64 %idx.clamped
  %e.1 = extractelement <16 x i32> %ly, i64 %idx.clamped
  %res = add i32 %e.0, %e.1
  ret i32 %res
}

define void @dont_scalarize_atomic_multiple_extracts(ptr %p, ptr %q) {
  %load = load atomic <2 x i32>, ptr %p seq_cst, align 8
  %extract0 = extractelement <2 x i32> %load, i64 0
  %extract1 = extractelement <2 x i32> %load, i64 1
  store i32 %extract0, ptr %q, align 4
  %q1 = getelementptr inbounds nuw i8, ptr %q, i64 4
  store i32 %extract1, ptr %q1, align 4
  ret void
}

define void @unused_extract(ptr %p) {
  %load = load <4 x float>, ptr %p, align 8
  %shuffle0 = shufflevector <4 x float> zeroinitializer, <4 x float> %load, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 4, i32 1, i32 5>
  %shuffle1 = shufflevector <4 x float> %shuffle0, <4 x float> zeroinitializer, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 4, i32 poison, i32 poison>
  %extract = extractelement <4 x float> %load, i64 1
  ret void
}
